svg.title: Secure Boot 2011 certificate expiry - frozen DBX bootkit exposure - kill chain
svg.desc: Two-lane diagram. Left lane is the victim device pre-OS trust chain: the OEM Platform Key root of trust, the Microsoft KEK CA 2011 that expires June 24 2026 (critical forcing event), the DBX deny-list that freezes forever on a non-migrated device so no future bootkit revocation can land (critical), a signed-but-revoked bootloader written to the EFI System Partition, pre-OS code execution that bypasses Secure Boot while the OS still reports it enabled (critical), a bootkit that persists in the ESP and NVRAM and survives OS reinstall, and the disabling of HVCI and BitLocker so the system boots green. Right lane is the attacker and bootkit-ecosystem operation: target a device still on the 2011 KEK, gain admin or physical access, drop a Microsoft-signed revoked .efi to the ESP (critical), ride the frozen DBX past Secure Boot with the revocation that never arrived (critical), persist below the OS where reimaging cannot reach, and blind EDR. Detection anchors are reading the UEFI variables directly rather than the OS Secure Boot boolean, ESP boot-component writes by a non-servicing process, the MicrosoftUpdateManagedOptIn registry state, and PCR7 Measured Boot changes.
svg.text: Secure Boot 2011 cert expiry - frozen DBX bootkit exposure
Vendor: Microsoft / NSA / Eclypsium · Date: 2026-06-02 · KEK CA 2011 expires 2026-06-24 · Day 41
VICTIM CHAIN - device pre-OS trust (PK -> KEK -> DB/DBX)
ATTACKER - bootkit ecosystem operation
OEM Platform Key (PK) - root of trust
PK owned by the OEM (Dell/HP/Lenovo/Supermicro)
Authorizes the KEK; NOT affected by the expiry
Chain: PK -> KEK -> {DB allow, DBX deny}
T1195.003 Compromise Hardware Supply Chain
A leaked PK = PKfail (CVE-2024-8105)
KEK CA 2011 expires Jun 24 2026
KEK = credential Windows Update uses to push
new DB allow-list and DBX deny-list entries
Once expired on a non-migrated host: no updates
Microsoft Corporation KEK CA 2011
CRITICAL: revocation-channel forcing event
DBX deny-list frozen forever
Device keeps the DB/DBX state it had at expiry
No future bootkit revocation can ever land
Only an OEM firmware update can unfreeze it
UEFI CA 2011 (DB) expires Jun 27 2026
CRITICAL: BlackLotus/BootHole stay un-revoked
Revoked signed bootloader on the ESP
Signed-but-revoked .efi written under \EFI\
DBX would normally block it - but it is frozen
bootmgfw rolled back or reloader.efi dropped
\EFI\Microsoft\Boot\bootmgfw.efi
T1553.006 Code Signing Policy Modification
Pre-OS code execution (bypass)
Vulnerable signed loader runs untrusted code
before the kernel - Secure Boot still says ON
T1542.003 Bootkit
CRITICAL: trust chain intact, control lost
Bootkit persists below the OS
Implant lives in the ESP / NVRAM, runs first
Survives OS reinstall and full disk wipe
Removal needs ESP rebuild + firmware re-flash
T1542.003 Bootkit / T1014 Rootkit
EDR runs ABOVE it - cannot see it
HVCI / BitLocker off, system boots green
Implant disables HVCI, BitLocker, Defender ELAM
then loads an unsigned kernel driver
OS reports Secure Boot enabled the whole time
T1562.001 Impair Defenses
Confirm-SecureBootUEFI returns True (lying)
A
Target a frozen-KEK device
Pick a host still on KEK CA 2011 after Jun 24
EOL hardware with no OEM firmware update
or any device that skipped the 2023 rollover
Scanners and EDR cannot enumerate the KEK
Audit UEFI variables, not the OS boolean
uefi.org/revocationlistfile = the baseline
B
Gain admin/SYSTEM or physical
Bootkit is post-access: commodity malware,
phishing, valid creds, or brief physical access
Needed to write the ESP and stage the loader
CVE-2025-3052 lowered the bar (signed util)
Any initial-access technique
Bootkit cements access - does not grant it
C
Drop a revoked-but-signed .efi
Write a Microsoft-signed vulnerable component
to the EFI System Partition (FAT32, \EFI\)
reloader.efi loads any unsigned payload
Bombshell: signed UEFI shell NULLs gSecurity2
CVE-2024-7344 reloader.efi (HISTORICAL)
CRITICAL: signed + frozen DBX = bypass
D
Ride frozen DBX past Secure Boot
The revocation that would stop it never arrived
Loader executes pre-OS with full trust
gSecurity2 NULL disables LoadImage checks
Windows keeps reporting Secure Boot ON
T1542.001 Pre-OS Boot: System Firmware
CRITICAL: only a DBX update could stop this
E
Persist in the ESP / NVRAM
Implant outside the OS volume and the disk
Reimaging the OS does not remove it
Loads on every boot before any defender
Eradication = ESP rebuild + firmware re-flash
T1542.003 Bootkit persistence
Reimage alone is insufficient
F
Blind EDR / anti-forensics
EDR and the OS run above the implant
PCR7 / Measured Boot is the only OS-side tell
HVCI/BitLocker disabled before they initialize
Ground truth: Get-SecureBootUEFI db/dbx/KEK
Detect below the OS, not from within it
The OS "Secure Boot is on" boolean is not integrity - ground truth lives in the UEFI variables (PK/KEK/db/dbx). Instrument those.
Revocation, not patching, remediates the boot layer. Cut the KEK (Jun 24 2026) and you cut the only field fix for the whole bootkit class.
Sequence: apply the OEM firmware update BEFORE the Windows Update cert change, or a CMOS clear / board RMA can strand the device.
Below-OS implants survive reimaging and blind EDR - detect at ESP integrity and PCR7, eradicate with an ESP rebuild + firmware re-flash.
